topic.what: Information-geometric framework for LLM stability analysis under entropic stress using composite scoring with entropy damping
topic.how: Integrates task utility, entropy, and internal structural proxies (internal integration, aligned reflective capacity) into damping-based stability formula
topic.why_matters: Standard accuracy benchmarks insufficient for reliability assessment under uncertainty; framework provides interpretable lens for stress-testing LLMs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean gain between generalized E* and reduced stability E formulations
characteristics.vs_baseline: improvement over reduced utility-entropy baseline E
characteristics.evidence: paired t-test p<0.001, Wilcoxon p<0.001, 95% CI [0.0247, 0.0351]
characteristics.context: Observed in all 80 model-scenario observations across 4 LLMs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduced-form stability score E = utility minus entropy
characteristics.context: Mean across 80 observations; ranged from 0.7827 to 0.9956

characteristics.what_it_measures: Entropic-damped stability score E* with internal structural moderation
characteristics.vs_baseline: exceeds reduced score in all cases
characteristics.context: Lower dispersion than baseline; ranged from 0.8532 to 0.9974

characteristics.what_it_measures: Negative association between entropy and stability
characteristics.context: Confirms entropy destabilizes model outputs under stress conditions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Association between stability gain and entropy level
characteristics.context: Larger improvements under higher entropy; algebraic structure of formulation
